messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: Trying to convince Seeed Studio to offer the Solar Node P1 Pro with this WiFi setup instead of the NRF52840 and GPS module so it could be an Observer. Mine arrives Friday and will probably order the ESP32. Would need to replace the NRF52840 (if it's plugged in with header pins - if soldered to main board that will be a pain). Any chance of FW for this? (edited)
messages.content_html: Trying to convince Seeed Studio to offer the Solar Node P1 Pro with this WiFi setup instead of the NRF52840 and GPS module so it could be an Observer. Mine arrives Friday and will probably order the ESP32. Would need to replace the NRF52840 (if it's plugged in with header pins - if soldered to main board that will be a pain). Any chance of FW for this? (edited)

messages.author: Art-X
messages.text: I wrote firmware for the ESP32-Wroom and NRF52840, both with sx1276. It was doable with some help of claude AI and I have both running. I can imagine you can do the same yourself for your hardware? (edited)
messages.content_html: I wrote firmware for the ESP32-Wroom and NRF52840, both with sx1276. It was doable with some help of claude AI and I have both running. I can imagine you can do the same yourself for your hardware? (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: No that's a bit above my paygrade. I can code php and js but firmware is on a different level. I see flasher has Xiao S3 WIO but that just has BLE unless you plug in the Wio SX1262. Does the FW recognize the SX1262 if connected? Is that your FW on flasher? If so, should be good to go with that as the ESP+SX will replace the NRF in the P1. (edited)
messages.content_html: No that's a bit above my paygrade. I can code php and js but firmware is on a different level. I see flasher has Xiao S3 WIO but that just has BLE unless you plug in the Wio SX1262. Does the FW recognize the SX1262 if connected? Is that your FW on flasher? If so, should be good to go with that as the ESP+SX will replace the NRF in the P1. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: I have cloned the MeshCore repo Modified the LilyGo Supreme platform.ini and created a wifi section Built and flashed and it's working. Now for the ESP version of the SenseCAP Solar: Under Variants I renamed the sensecap_solar folder to sensecap_solar_nrf and added NRF to each env: section in platform.ini so there are unique build names for NRF and ESP variants. Then I copied the sensecap_solar_nrf folder to a sensecap_solar_esp folder In sensecap_solar_esp/platform.ini I replaced NRF with ESP in each env: section Now I need to replace the NRF parts with ESP parts in platform.ini and make some changes in the SenseCapSolarBoard.cpp file (and others?) since it refers to BLE This is the point where I'm going to have to ask for some guidance or help from those much more experienced to finish this since compiling FW for LoRa boards is all new to me. Hopefully I've laid the groundwork for the two variants and just need to finish the ESP part. I think it will be a quick task and we'll have a new variant to support the SenseCAP Solar P1 with ESP32S3 for WiFi access to MQTT. (edited)
messages.content_html: I have cloned the MeshCore repo Modified the LilyGo Supreme platform.ini and created a wifi section Built and flashed and it's working. Now for the ESP version of the SenseCAP Solar: Under Variants I renamed the sensecap_solar folder to sensecap_solar_nrf and added NRF to each env: section in platform.ini so there are unique build names for NRF and ESP variants. Then I copied the sensecap_solar_nrf folder to a sensecap_solar_esp folder In sensecap_solar_esp/platform.ini I replaced NRF with ESP in each env: section Now I need to replace the NRF parts with ESP parts in platform.ini and make some changes in the SenseCapSolarBoard.cpp file (and others?) since it refers to BLE This is the point where I'm going to have to ask for some guidance or help from those much more experienced to finish this since compiling FW for LoRa boards is all new to me. Hopefully I've laid the groundwork for the two variants and just need to finish the ESP part. I think it will be a quick task and we'll have a new variant to support the SenseCAP Solar P1 with ESP32S3 for WiFi access to MQTT. (edited)

messages.author: Augeas
messages.text: Does the Xiao ESP32S3 build listen on the UART bridge? I've flashed it to the USB companion firmware and tried prodding it from a Pico board using the MicroPython bindings, but so far it seems dormant. https://github.com/fdlamotte/meshcore_upy
messages.content_html: Does the Xiao ESP32S3 build listen on the UART bridge? I've flashed it to the USB companion firmware and tried prodding it from a Pico board using the MicroPython bindings, but so far it seems dormant. https://github.com/fdlamotte/meshcore_upy

messages.author: Augeas
messages.text: I'm guessing that's a "No"? All references to RS232 are commented out, and SERIAL_TX is only defined in companion_radio_serial: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/blob/bfd4800f5985049be1cbb8785d129e7680b89771/variants/xiao_s3_wio/platformio.ini

messages.author: Augeas
messages.text: Thought it might be a slightly Jolly Wheeze to get a hacker camp badge to work as a MC client. MT has actual documentation about using its protbufs via UART, but its all so bloody overcomplex and janky that I Just Cant Be Arsed.
